frames.caption: Add 1 to the number stored as a digit array. Sweep from the last digit, carrying left when a 9 rolls over to 0.
frames.note: The array [9, 9] represents the integer 99. Adding 1 gives 100, which needs three digits — so this canonical example showcases the all-9s case where the result grows by one. We never convert to a real integer (it could overflow); we manipulate the digits directly.

frames.caption: i = 1: inspect digit 9. Is it < 9?
frames.note: We walk the digit array right to left, exactly the way you add by hand: the ones place first, then tens, then hundreds. A single carry can only propagate leftward, so one pass suffices — there is no need to revisit a digit once we have moved past it.

frames.caption: digits[1] is 9, so set it to 0 and carry left.
frames.note: A 9 cannot hold one more, so it rolls over to 0 and the carry continues to the next digit on the left — just like 9 + 1 = 10 forces a carry. We keep sweeping; if the very next digit is also a 9 it too becomes 0, and so on.

frames.caption: i = 0: inspect digit 9. Is it < 9?
frames.note: We walk the digit array right to left, exactly the way you add by hand: the ones place first, then tens, then hundreds. A single carry can only propagate leftward, so one pass suffices — there is no need to revisit a digit once we have moved past it.

frames.caption: digits[0] is 9, so set it to 0 and carry left.
frames.note: A 9 cannot hold one more, so it rolls over to 0 and the carry continues to the next digit on the left — just like 9 + 1 = 10 forces a carry. We keep sweeping; if the very next digit is also a 9 it too becomes 0, and so on.

frames.caption: Loop ended with carry still alive (all digits were 9). Allocate a new array of length 3, filled with 0s.
frames.note: The all-9s edge case: the carry never died, so it falls off the left end. The number gained a digit (99 + 1 = 100), so we allocate an array ONE longer, put the surviving 1 in front, and the trailing zeros are already there by default. This is the one input where Plus One changes the array length.

frames.caption: Put the surviving carry as 1 in front: [9, 9] + 1 = [1, 0, 0]. Return the longer array.
frames.note: The all-9s edge case: the carry never died, so it falls off the left end. The number gained a digit (99 + 1 = 100), so we allocate an array ONE longer, put the surviving 1 in front, and the trailing zeros are already there by default. This is the one input where Plus One changes the array length.
